images.copyright: Minarets of Charminar, Hyderabad, Telangana (© RAJARSHI SENGUPTA/iStock/Getty Images)
images.title: Making a state-ment
images.caption: Telangana Day
images.desc: At midnight, a map shifted—and a region found its rhythm. On 2 June 2014, Telangana became India's youngest state, carved from Andhra Pradesh, with Hyderabad as its capital. Telangana Day marks that moment—and the momentum since.

og.title: Telangana Day
og.desc: At midnight, a map shifted—and a region found its rhythm. On 2 June 2014, Telangana became India's youngest state, carved from Andhra…

images.copyright: Palermo skyline, Sicily, Italy (© Sean Pavone/Getty Images)
images.title: Flavours of history
images.caption: Palermo, Sicily, Italy
images.desc: Palermo, the capital of the island of Sicily in southern Italy, is a city where layers of history, art and culture meet in a vivid and sometimes chaotic harmony. Founded by the Phoenicians and later shaped by Romans, Arabs, Normans and Spaniards, it carries traces of many civilisations that passed through the Mediterranean. This rich past is visible in its architecture, from the Arab-Norman palaces and churches recognised by UNESCO to the Baroque facades that define its historic streets.

og.desc: Palermo, the capital of the island of Sicily in southern Italy, is a city where layers of history, art and culture meet in a vivid and…

images.copyright: Highway through Xitai Jinaier Lake, Qinghai Province, China (© Kaicheng Xu/Getty Images)
images.title: Between the blues
images.caption: Highway through Xitai Jinaier Lake, Qinghai Province, China
images.desc: Cutting a surreal path through the high-altitude deserts of western China, Xitai Jinaier Lake (also known as West Taijinar Lake) is a striking salt lake in the remote Qaidam Basin. A stretch of National Highway 315 runs directly across its shallow waters, creating the illusion of a road floating between two worlds. On one side, the water often appears deep blue; on the other, a softer green hue, a contrast shaped by differing mineral concentrations in the brine.

og.title: Highway through Xitai Jinaier Lake, Qinghai Province, China

images.copyright: Plains zebra foal in Etosha National Park, Namibia (© Sharon Heald/Nature Picture Library)
images.title: Reading between the lines
images.caption: Plains zebra foal in Etosha National Park, Namibia
images.desc: Etosha National Park, Namibia, is not a place for soft starts, and the plains zebra foal in this image seems to get the memo. In a land this vast—nearly 22,300 square kilometres of salt pans and grassland—timing is everything, and hesitation is expensive. Lions, spotted hyenas, wild dogs, cheetahs and leopards constantly pressure the herd—especially the youngest—so zebras are born ready. Minutes after arrival, they're upright. Hours later, they're moving.

og.title: Plains zebra foal in Etosha National Park, Namibia

images.copyright: Mount Everest summit, Sagarmatha National Park, Nepal (© fotoVoyager/Getty Images)
images.title: A 'peak' into history
images.caption: 1953 Mount Everest Expedition
images.desc: In Sagarmatha National Park, Nepal, Mount Everest rises into view, calm and unmoved as evening settles in. But on 29 May, 1953, that stillness was broken. After years of planning and weeks of climbing, New Zealander Edmund Hillary and Sherpa mountaineer Tenzing Norgay reached the summit. At more than 8,800 metres above sea level, they became the first people known to have stood at Earth's highest point.

og.title: 1953 Mount Everest Expedition

images.copyright: Old city wall of Hwaseong Fortress, Suwon, South Korea (© aomam/Getty Images)
images.title: Design meets discipline
images.caption: Old city wall of Hwaseong Fortress, Suwon, South Korea
images.desc: As night settles over Suwon, South Korea, the old city wall pictured here shows that function doesn't necessarily cancel out beauty—it can create it. Built between 1794 and 1796 under King Jeongjo of the Joseon Dynasty, Hwaseong Fortress was more than a barrier. It was policy in stone: a reform-minded push for a leaner, better-run state, driven by practicality and a deeply personal motive. Jeongjo ordered its construction to honour his father while strengthening a kingdom that wanted smarter systems, not just thicker walls.

images.copyright: Sea otter, Kachemak Bay, Homer, Alaska, United States (© roclwyr/Getty Images)
images.title: No 'otter' like them
images.caption: World Otter Day
images.desc: On World Otter Day, just look at this cutie swimming in the waters of Kachemak Bay, Alaska! Otters are captivating creatures that play an essential role in maintaining healthy ecosystems. Supported by the Otter Specialist Group (OSG)—a global network of professionals dedicated to the conservation of all 14 species—this day highlights the otter's vital contribution to nature. Known for their playful behaviour and remarkable intelligence, otters are key indicators of waterway health. Sadly, they face growing challenges from habitat loss and pollution.

images.copyright: Lupine flowers in bloom, Northern California, United States (© Jeffrey Lewis/Tandem Stills + Motion)
images.title: A season written in lupine
images.caption: Lupine flowers in bloom, Northern California, United States
images.desc: As spring sweeps across Northern California in the United States, the landscape is transformed by the vibrant displays of lupine flowers in shades of blue, purple and white. Flourishing in the region's Mediterranean climate, these wildflowers provide a vital source of nectar for pollinators such as bees, butterflies and beetles. With over 200 species found worldwide, lupines range from coastal varieties to those that thrive in mountain meadows. Beyond their striking beauty, lupines are essential to the ecosystem: by fixing nitrogen underground, they enrich soil fertility and support the growth of surrounding plants, even in nutrient-poor environments.
